opts: Skate with maximum effort every single shift — the right plays will happen on their own for you
Know your specific job for the next 30 seconds — faceoff role, where to go, who to cover
Try to score the first time you touch the puck — every shot must go on net now
Get pumped, skate fast, and win the next faceoff through pure energy and intensity
Follow the opposing center all the way back to your zone to stay matched up
Hold the blue line but cheat back slightly — be ready if the puck turns over
Ignore the breaking center completely and focus on keeping the puck in deep
Call loudly for the puck so you can step in and take a hard shot from the point
Stop, hold the puck, and read how the scrambling defenders start to set up now
Attack the scrambled D immediately — they haven't recovered. Be the second wave.
Pass the puck back to your own D and reset up top before attacking the net
Skate into the corner with the puck and protect it along the boards to cycle play
Try a tricky deke on the goalie since the defenseman is still trailing behind
Shoot as quickly as possible once you're in range — don't let the D recover
Pull up at the top of the circles and wait for your trailing teammate to join
Slow down a little to catch your breath before you attack the net with a play
Give a quick slash at their stick to make them leave the crease area
Get your body low and push them away from the crease — no penalty
Skate behind your net and yell to your goalie to push the player out
Lift their stick up high and hold it away from any incoming rebound
Freeze the puck and let your team take a defensive zone faceoff instead
Pass to the winger coming back — beat the forecheck before they can set up
Rim the puck around the boards past the three forecheckers to the far side
Shoot the puck all the way down the ice to the far end and change lines
Stay weak side in case the shot goes wide and rims back around to you
Drive hard to the net — be there for the rebound on the weak side
Hold at the half-wall in case the puck comes back out to your side
Back up to the blue line to help your D in case of a quick turnover
Win it backward to your own defenseman so they can start a clean clear play
Win it forward — kick it toward the corner or boards to prevent the PP shot
It doesn't matter where the puck goes as long as you win the faceoff
Tie up the opposing center's stick right after the puck drops to lock him
Goalie freezes the puck for a defensive zone faceoff — three forecheckers makes the breakout attempt too dangerous
Goalie finds the center breaking through the middle or a winger escaping — don't give the puck to a covered player
Goalie shoots the puck hard down the ice for a long clear past their blue line and forces them to fully reset
Goalie rims the puck around the boards behind the net so the far-side winger can pick it up on the way around it
Commit hard to the left-side puck carrier — step up and take away the shot before they cross
Back up fast enough to not get beaten by their speed, stay in the middle, and force the shooter
Race back to your crease to help your goalie on the shot coming from the left-side puck carrier's side
Skate to the weak side and cover the off-wing attacker — take away the back door and let the goalie shoot
Settled the puck for the perfect shot — pick the top corner before you release the wrist shot
Released immediately on the catch — the opening was there when you first received it
Passed to an open teammate — don't shoot unless the look is clean, moving it opens a better chance
Skated closer to the net — get to the top of the crease where the goalie has less net to cover
Slow the pace of play so your own tired forwards can rest their legs for a minute
Keep cycling and creating traffic — tired defensemen make mistakes under sustained
Shoot from every angle no matter where you are — just put lots of pucks on the net
Change lines right away before the tired defenders can recover their wind
Keep cycling patiently and wait for the clogged slot to finally clear up for a scoring chance
Pass back to the open point — four defenders below the dots means the point shot is uncontested
Shoot from the corner no matter what the angle is and hope for a lucky bounce off the goalie
Dump the puck back into the corner, change lines, and come back with fresher legs on next shift
Commit to the puck carrier on the left — step up hard and take away the shot before they look across
Stay in the middle — take away the pass to the cutter and force the shot. Your goalie handles the shot.
Chase the cutter to the right — take away the pass and the tip and let your goalie stop the shot on the left
Back up all the way into your crease — give your goalie the best sight line and let them handle the 2-on-1
Set up the power play again — your PP unit has been working, so reset in the zone and run it one more time
Get the puck to a breaking teammate immediately — the 2-on-2 counter is better than resetting the PP
Hold the puck along the boards and regroup — let your power play catch up and set up a clean zone entry
Dump the puck in deep and chase — get it behind their defense and forecheck with fresh power play legs
Yes — pinch hard and crash down on the puck before the defender can gain clean control
No — if possession is about to be lost, pinching puts you deep with no coverage during
Go to the net front instead — crash the crease and look for a rebound while your forward battles
Take a quick shot from the line as a distraction — firing anything on net gets everyone to shift
Stop and call loudly for a penalty — the refs will see the stick hold and the push from behind
Keep your feet moving and get your stick free — any shot or swipe on net is worth taking
Fall down hard to draw the penalty — going to the ice with two defenders on you gets a whistle
Pass the puck out of the slot area to a teammate at the point or half-wall and reset the play
Force the pass to the covered left winger and hope they win it
Reverse — walk behind the net and find the open right side
Rim the puck around the boards to the far side winger
Hold it behind the net and wait for the forechecker to give up
Shoot right away because your coach is yelling for you to shoot the puck now
Pass to the better position — the right play is still the right play
Hold the puck and take a second to think about your options
Skate to the bench for a quick line change with fresh legs
Keep cycling — eventually the defenders will make a mistake if you're patient enough
Stop cycling — dump the puck deep, change lines, and come back with a new entry
Shoot from the corner hard and hope the goalie gives up a big rebound out front
Pass the puck back to your D at the point for a hard shot through all the traffic
Dump the puck deep into the corner and chase it hard while the clock is winding down
Shoot from wherever you are — any shot is better than nothing with 10 seconds left
Skate the puck back out, regroup, and try to set up a clean zone entry one more time
Hold the puck along the boards and protect it until the power play clock runs all out
Keep skating toward the net — get back to help your goalie on the slap shot coverage
Get your stick in the shooting lane to deflect or block — you're close enough
Drop to the ice and slide hard to block the slap shot with your body
Call for your D to rush the shooter — yell to step up and challenge the slap shot
You go yourself — you're the highest forward so the rimmed puck is your responsibility to get
The near winger goes — they're in better position. You stay high and be the next pass option.
Both forwards race to the boards together so one of you wins the race to the rimmed puck
The D jumps up from below the dots to retrieve the rimmed puck on the far side and move it
Shoot because you're in the slot — the slot is the best shooting area, so any puck from there goes on net
Pass to the breaking teammate — open player at the back post with goalie unaware is the highest-percentage play
Hold the puck one extra second on your stick — wait for the defender to commit, then make your read right there
Skate behind the net first — use the net as a pick on the defender and come out on your forehand side
Stay deep on your goal line and let the shooter come all the way in
Skate out to the top of the crease — cut the angle and take away net
Skate all the way out past the hash marks to challenge the shooter early
Drop into your butterfly early and seal the ice to take away the low shot
Cheat over toward the open passing option on the far side of the slot
Set for the shot — your D is taking away the pass, you take the shot
Rush straight out hard at the puck carrier to challenge him aggressively
Come out past the top of your crease to cut down the shooter's angle more
Give up on the shot since you can't see it — cover the low corners and hope the puck hits you for a save
Move laterally to find a gap around the screener, call to your D to move them, and be ready for a tip
Drop into butterfly early to cover the bottom half — most screened shots go low so seal the ice completely
Stay completely still and trust your positioning — any movement takes you out of the angle, hold your spot
Back toward the shooter so they can take another shot quickly
To the right corner — opposite side from where the shot came
Straight up in the air so the whistle blows and play can stop
Anywhere in front of the net so a teammate can get to it first
Try to carry the puck out yourself around the net and up the ice past the forechecker
Stop it for your left defenseman who is coming to get it — get the puck to their forehand
Shoot the puck the length of the ice and let your forwards chase it down on the forecheck
Freeze the puck quickly and wait for the whistle and a defensive zone faceoff draw play
